title: Venue Partners — Comeketo Catering
purpose: Master venue catalog: every location Comeketo has catered or is actively working. Captures partnership tier, capacity, kitchen/bar/grill capabilities, pricing band, distance from kitchen, assigned consultant, and CRM links. This is the source of truth for 'have we worked there,' 'who owns that relationship,' and 'can we operate this venue.'

source.file: rawdata/Venue Partners( frequency of jobs there ) 04_08 - Venues -Job Complete _won deal upcoming.csv
source.import_note: CSV had free-text multi-line 'Characteristics' cells mixing kitchen/bar/capacity/pricing notes. Website column was offset — many rows had mis-aligned URLs. Parsed the signal fields and kept raw characteristic text per-venue; ignored the offset URL column except where unambiguous.

tier_vocabulary.tier_1_connected_partner: Active working relationship. Recurring events. Known operational quirks.
tier_vocabulary.tier_2_preferred_partner: Listed or positioned as a preferred vendor at the venue, OR positioned in Comeketo's top recommendation list. Warmest tier.
tier_vocabulary.tier_3_growth_partner: Growing relationship — events happening but partnership not yet formalized either direction.
tier_vocabulary.untiered: Has worked an event but no formal partnership structure declared yet.

Rhon.notes: Account manager on Arrow Head Acres.

Bibi.notes: Account manager on WCU Littleton — her personal relationship with the bank contact is the anchor.

tier_1_connected_partners.name: Charles River Museum
tier_1_connected_partners.kitchen: Residential style, good space to work
tier_1_connected_partners.characteristics: Huge venue. Very demanding. Take trash with us. Sweep the floor. Poor parking for guests. We do everything here. Not that flexible to drop off things a day before but we can still double check.

tier_1_connected_partners.name: Commanders Mansion
tier_1_connected_partners.characteristics: Historical house. VERY VERY VERY STRICT. Special treatment. We need to do everything: chair and table placement, bussing. No bar service.

tier_1_connected_partners.name: Garage B Events at The Speedway
tier_1_connected_partners.kitchen: Kitchen space but no equipment. Space for cooking and prep.
tier_1_connected_partners.characteristics: No bar. People usually have money. Boston area. Big space. Nice outdoor but city limitations.

tier_1_connected_partners.name: Knights & Ladies of Kaleva
tier_1_connected_partners.characteristics: Close to our kitchen. Lake front view. Good to work with — can drop off ahead of time. Indoor and outdoor. Ceremony outside, food should be indoor in most cases.

tier_1_connected_partners.name: Mechanics Hall
tier_1_connected_partners.kitchen: Cook at loading dock, not inside
tier_1_connected_partners.characteristics: Big classic venue. Gala parties. High-end. No established good relationship yet — open terrain.

tier_1_connected_partners.name: Cathedral of Pines

tier_1_connected_partners.upcoming: June 26, 2025 and August 31, 2025 (Brenna Arnold, Gladi Matos)
tier_1_connected_partners.preferred_vendor_status: Application submitted, will be reviewed after first event.

tier_1_connected_partners.name: Pierce House

tier_1_connected_partners.name: Hardwick Winery

known_data_issues: Website-link column in source was offset across many rows, attaching the wrong URL to each venue. Only URLs that unambiguously match the venue name were kept; others were dropped rather than preserved incorrectly.
Lilac Hedge Farm appears twice (rows 380, 403) — collapsed into single untiered entry; preserved the later date (6/21/2025).
Crystal Ballroom appears twice (one with address, one without) — kept the populated row.
Mistletoe Acres Tree Farm appears twice (one marked COMPLETED, one blank duplicate) — collapsed.
Camp Robindel, Community Harvest Project, Night Shift Brewing have no completed events in CSV but are in the list — kept as future-opportunity records.
Grton School Campus Historic Barn flagged 'they are closed already' in source — captured in partnered_untiered with closed status.
Some Andre venues have multiple Close CRM URLs attached because the same physical venue was created as separate CRM leads per event. Preserved the list; future-pass consolidation may be warranted.
downstream_hooks: projects/ could host per-top-venue project records if deal cadence deserves it. Monoosnock (13 events, Toni's top) is the obvious first candidate — a dedicated relationship project with monthly-touch cadence would keep the 13-event streak compounding.
Brazilian-owned venues (The Hacienda, Viewpoint Venue) align with Comeketo's brand positioning — worth a separate tagged view.

Corporate accounts (Black Bear Concrete, ETS, WCU, House of Reps, AIS HQ) have DIFFERENT cadence than wedding venues (monthly touch, not 3-touchpoint-per-month). When Rodbot drafts outreach, it should branch on corporate vs venue character.
Red-flag venue (Metrowest Painting) should never auto-surface in 'venues to contact' suggestions without a warning.

Bibi's WCU relationship is an example of account-manager pattern distinct from consultant-owner — the scorecard owner is Andre but the active relationship runs through Bibi. Any agent outreach must route through Bibi, not directly.
